title: Horizontal Motions
intro: Moving one character at a time is slow. `w` jumps forward one word, `b` jumps back, `e` jumps to a word's end. `0` jumps to the start of the line, `$` to the end. `f{char}` jumps forward onto the next occurrence of a character.
summary: Word jumps, line starts, and line ends.

exercises.instruction: Jump to the start of the word `error` using `w`.
hints: `w` also stops on punctuation groups like `(` and `//`.

exercises.instruction: Jump to the end of the line with `$`.
hints: `$` jumps straight to the last character of the line.

exercises.instruction: Now jump back to the start of the line with `0`.
hints: `0` jumps to column zero, no matter where you are.

exercises.instruction: Jump onto the opening parenthesis using `f(`.
hints: `f` waits for a character, then jumps onto its next occurrence.

exercises.instruction: Jump backwards to the start of the word `three` using `b`.
hints: Each press of `b` jumps back one word.

exercises.instruction: Land on the `f` of `four` — any keys allowed. Try `$` then `b`, or three presses of `w`.
hints: `$` jumps to the end of the line, `b` backs up to the start of the word.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is at the end of the line. Press `0` to return to the start, then `f*` to land on the `*`.
Land on the `t` of `it`: `w` to reach the word, then `e` to jump to its end.
hints: `e` jumps to the last character of the current or next word.
